text.title: aws-architecture-services Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-architecture-services as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running all workloads as Lambda including a websocket server, ML inference at >5GB model size, or a 20-minute nightly report.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lambda has a 15-minute timeout, no persistent connections, no GPU support, and a 10GB ephemeral storage cap.

failure-mode.description: Using SNS as a queue replacement for a single consumer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SNS has no retention — if the consumer is down, messages are lost. Use SQS for single-consumer reliable delivery.

failure-mode.description: Overly permissive IAM with wildcard (*) resource ARNs in production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wildcards grant unintended access to all resources, violating least privilege and failing security audits.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An AZ outage takes the entire workload offline. Multi-AZ is required for any SLA above 99.9%.
